text: I arrive as open-source contributor deciding whether a project is worth a first PR. My past: I have wasted weekends on inactive repositories and now look for maintainers, docs, issues, and contribution signals before investing time.
My screen is Laptop 15 (1440x900, desktop).
Today I want to: Find whether GitHub helps me judge repository credibility and contribution paths quickly.
My life goal is: Build a public technical reputation without wasting years on dead projects.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

I think: I landed on a polished homepage, but it is selling the platform more than helping me judge a real project yet.
My reaction: I like the energy, but for my goal I need repository signals, not a broad promise about collaboration.
Idea: Give newcomers a fast path to assess a project’s activity, maintainer responsiveness, and first-contribution options.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life is worth it only if the next step gets me closer to credible projects with clear contribution paths.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that this screen builds brand confidence but gives me low evidence for choosing where to contribute. Confidence: high.
My impulse is to explore.
I think: Something about this moment makes me want to take one more step instead of leaving.
I follow a hunch and check /pricing.

I think: I landed on pricing, not the kind of evidence I need to judge whether a project is active or welcoming.
My reaction: My reaction is mild impatience because the page is polished, but it pushes plans before helping me evaluate contribution paths.
Idea: Give contributors a fast credibility view with maintainer activity, issue quality, and first-contribution signals.
Life-cost decision: One more action is worth 1 month because finding the repository view could directly support my goal of building reputation without wasting years.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that GitHub probably can help me judge projects, but this screen is not useful for that; confidence 0.74.
